name: Anirban Bandyopadhyay
dates_notes: Living materials scientist (National Institute for Materials Science, Tsukuba); birth year approximate.
core_thesis: Anirban Bandyopadhyay is a contemporary physicist — Principal Researcher at the National Institute for Materials Science (Tsukuba, Japan), an experimental collaborator in the Penrose–Hameroff Orch-OR program, and author of *Nanobrain* (2020) — who proposes the first formalization of the three *guṇas* of classical Sāṅkhya since Vijñānabhikṣu. He treats Sāṅkhya as a metaphysics of form to be taken literally: *prakṛti* really transforms (*pariṇāma*), *puruṣa* really witnesses, and the *guṇas* are real constituents of the manifest world rather than psychological or moral categories. On his mapping the three *guṇas* are physical operations on a single mathematical primitive, the Phase Prime Metric (PPM), which he identifies with *puruṣa*: *sattva* is projection from infinity, *rajas* is the transformation of one geometric shape into another at a singularity, and *tamas* is the bonding of shapes at undefined points — one substrate carrying three simultaneous modes, with consciousness as their co-presence. The reading sets Īśvarakṛṣṇa's *Sāṅkhya-Kārikā* (with Gauḍapāda and Vācaspati Miśra) and the *Yoga-Sūtra* of Patañjali with Vyāsa's *Bhāṣya* in counterpoint with his time-crystal and fractal-information mathematics, landing on real-transformation (*pariṇāma*) where Advaita reads the manifest as sublatable-not-null and Abhinavagupta reads the substrate as one-self-standing.

cross_engagements.brief: Abhinavagupta presses the same substrate structure axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `one-self-standing` rather than `many-coordinate-reals`.
Adi Sankara presses the same manifestation status axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `sublatable-not-null` rather than `real-transformation`.
Anandabodha presses the same selfhood structure axis in the phenomenological register, but with the value `witness-self` rather than `substantial-self`.
Alfred North Whitehead presses the same temporal mode axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `process-primary` rather than `substance-primary`.
Akalanka presses the same practice-path axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `meditative-discipline` rather than `knowledge-discipline`.
Anantakrishna Sastri presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `identity-with-ground` rather than `isolation-or-discriminative-release`.
Baladeva Vidyabhushana presses the same causation model axis in the metaphysical register, but with the value `unchanged-ground-with-changing-power` rather than `real-transformation`.
